fieldInfo.sessionExited: Whether the CLI process has exited. Continuing the conversation spawns a new process via the resume command.
fieldInfo.seen: Whether you have viewed this session's latest output; drives the unread marker in the sidebar.
fieldInfo.updatedAt: When this session's database record was last written. Any field change (title, status, archive, ...) refreshes it.
fieldInfo.lastInteractedAt: When you last sent input or interacted with the terminal in this session — unlike record updated, which background writes also refresh.

sessionAutoRename.claude: Claude Code's built-in auto-naming is English-only; Yoda generates a localized title and overrides it.

sessionStateSync.claude: Early versions inferred state from JSONL transcripts, which left zombie "working" sessions; switching to PID-based detection made it solid.
sessionStateSync.codex: Porting the PID-based approach — currently in testing.

usageSync.claude: Reads the real plan tier from the keychain and computes cost with ccusage-compatible accounting — no fabricated numbers.

skills.codex: Codex is stricter about skill format; Yoda's built-in validator surfaces issues right in the sidebar.

hooks.codex: Codex hooks went GA on 2026-05-14 (10 events + content-hash trust model), closely mirroring CC's event set — Yoda integration under research.

statusline.codex: Codex has a built-in statusline (tui.status_line in config.toml plus /statusline), though custom command-backed rendering isn't supported yet — Yoda integration under research.

usage.subtitle: Lifetime activity across all tasks and sessions — token burn parsed from agent transcripts.

caliber.tasks: How it's counted: tasks with a non-null archive date (archiving is the completion act). The sub-line is all tasks ever created.
caliber.tokens: How it's counted: parsed from local Claude / Codex session transcripts, including subagent transcripts and any auxiliary directories configured in project settings. Total = input + output + cache read + cache write. Other clients have no transcript reader and count as zero.
caliber.lines: How it's counted: per task = committed diff vs source branch (merge-base) + uncommitted working-tree changes. Live for active tasks; archived tasks use their last snapshot (tasks archived before this feature shipped have none and count as zero).
caliber.activeDays: How it's counted: local calendar days with any token usage in session transcripts.
caliber.heatmap: How it's counted: the grid shows the current calendar month plus the previous two. Each session's tokens are bucketed into the local date they were consumed. Color levels are quantiles (25/50/75) of non-zero days.
caliber.byProject: How it's counted: each session is attributed to its task's project; untracked auxiliary-directory sessions go to the project that configured the path (sessions tracked by another project stay there — the global total never double-counts).
caliber.byModel: How it's counted: each transcript row is attributed to its provider-reported model id (Claude: message.model; Codex: the active turn_context model). Rows without one count as unknown.
caliber.byRuntime: How it's counted: grouped by the client that ran each session; only sessions with parseable transcripts are included.
caliber.bySource: How it's counted: the account mode recorded when each session was spawned. Sessions from before tracking landed are attributed to the client's current setting (estimate).
caliber.topTasks: How it's counted: sum of all session tokens per task, top 10.

aiLogs.title: AI Logs
